rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY JEAN DAVIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Distribution Of Assets, Status On Distribution Of Reserve And Filing Of Receipts
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine what the status is of the $40,000 reserve the executor was authorized on 12/29/21 to withhold for "taxes, tax work" and other potential liabilities of the estate. No receipt has been filed by the sole residuary beneficiary. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARY GRACE KIMBROUGH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Establish First Party Special Needs Trust Under Substituted Judgement; Or In The Alternative; Under Prob C 2403 And 3602(D); For Reimbursement To Petitioners; For Approval Of Caregiver Payments To Family Members; And For Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/8/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) There are three limited conservators of the estate; only one needs to verify the petition, but all must sign. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(C). All must also execute the [proposed] SNT, even if just two of them will serve as the initial trustees. (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (Item 1 of the Notice of Hearing must specify the representative capacity of the petitioner(s).) (3) The time of the hearing was also missing from the Notice of Hearing. (4) The bond is missing from the petition caption. [CRC 7.102.] (Bond is rounded up, not down to the nearest $1,000.) (5) Unlike the hours set forth in Exhibit L for 2020 and 2021, none of the specific time billed and already paid are set forth in Exhibit M for care hours reportedly spent in 2022. (6) Exhibit M is also just the petitioners' list of purported reimbursements without any support. (7) There does not appear to be any order authorizing fees to any attorney but the petitioners have already paid Melanie Emmons and Mike Zatopa. (8) How was the missed work time for Jason determined? (Although the petitioners claim that they are not seeking reimbursement from the [proposed] SNT for their expenses, they have already reimbursed themselves the bulk of the expenses from the funds that should have been used to fund the SNT, as the amount returned to them from Leigh Law Group was due to the SNT.) (9) Who is the billing party with the initials KF and how was he/she qualified to bill? (10) No declaration in support of additional fees has been filed, as alleged in paragraph 84 of the petition. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/27/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Please do NOT provide copies of incomplete or unfiled papers; they will not be reviewed. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN BARRY BALDWIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: 12200 filed 12/1/22 Continued to 5/15/23. (No earlier available date.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice was not given to the party who filed a request for special notice on 6/17/21. (Only the withdrawal of the RSN filed by the CCSF was filed on 12/2/22.) (2) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir OR BENEFICIARY is or has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (3) Paragraph 18 of the petition is incomplete. (4) Why are predeceased beneficiaries entitled to receive their distribution? This examiner has been unable to find anything in the records indicating the dates of death of Richard Baldwin and Pippa Scolari; the dates were not set forth in the initial petitions. [LRSF 14.18 D.] (5) Another year is a long time to administer the estate, when the petitioners expected to file a petition for final distribution in the beginning of 2023. After procedural issues are addressed, an appearance will likely be required. Deadline for delivering courtesy copies of any responsive pleadings or proofs of service to the assigned examiner is 5/4/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/10/2023 (no earlier date available). 1) The DE-121 form filed on 03/22/2023 shows notice was given less than 15 days before the hearing. New notice must be given with a copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6). 2) The response filed on 03/21/2023 must be verified by the petitioner. 3) As the total amount of creditor claims on file exceeds the value of this estate estimated by the petitioner ($161,796), the bond amount required by LRSF 14.16(F) should be $162,000, unless the competing petitioner, as trustee of the devisee trust, waives the bond on the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-142. It should be noted that counsel for the competing petitioner, in his declaration filed on 09/26/2022, estimated that the estate value is $53,000. 3) Please describe reasonable efforts made to ascertain the decedent's mother and her whereabouts (or date of death). Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
